entry.title: Re: Function module parameter not found
entry.content: A parameter is local to its procedure (as you can see: "Local Interface"), you can't use it directly in another procedure, pass it explicitly via parameters (or to avoid, transfer it to a global function group parameter).

entry.title: SAP BTP Role with unrestricted attributes
entry.content:
Hello SAP CAP CDS Community,

I recently deployed in SAP BTP a CAP service with a role within xs-security.json with attribute having the setting "valueRequired": false in order to be created by default as unrestricted attribute.

According to the SAP Documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/application-security-descriptor-configuration-syntax#loio517895a9612241259d6941dbf9ad81cb__section_c1n_jfd_tkb in case of an unrestricted attribute the behavior should be the same as if the attribute would not exist for this role.

Unfourtunately, it is not the case. all the CUD operations are reporting 403 forbidden and only READ works and I can see all the entries in the list table.

In case that I specify that specific an static attribute value within the BTP role, than for the instances with that value everything works fine.

Do you experienced the same and maybe have a solution for me? Of course, on CAP service level, all entities are all annotated accordingly:

annotate...

{
        grant: ['*'],
        to   : ['DisassemblyManager'],
        where: 'name = $user.PlantGroup'
    }
Thanks in advance,
Catalin

entry.title: SoD without GRC Tool
entry.content:
Hi!

My question is a bit complex, is it possible for a company to create SoD risks without GRC tool?

I have an idea of how to have this, but I need help with ideas to automate this process and one north for starting this.

What comes to mind is to get a list of transactions that the functional/business area considers critical and define what cannot be with a single user, my point is how do I consolidate this by centralizing it in one place so that I can do simulations, for example of new access to a user and be able to identify if this can be assigned.

I know it sounds like an difficulty thing to do, but I want to at least try to have better risk control and be able to make improvements until the company acquires a GRC tool that does this type of work in an automated way.

 - ZPM003 (PM): Enables the mass upload of order confirmations into maintenance orders from a text file. (No image attached).

I would like to check whether there are any existing Standard transaction codes that could replace these functionalities. (My initial assessment is that there aren't).

Thanks in advance,

Sebastián

entry.title: Re: Different Inbound Delivery Document Type for Differen...
entry.content:
Hello
 
Please do following steps:
 
1. Create Custom STO Type:
  • Configuration:
    • Go to IMG -> Material Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order -> Define Document Types 
       
    • Create a new entry for your custom STO type 
       
    • Assign it to the item category "U" 
    • Define the relevant confirmation control key 
       
 
2. Create Custom Inbound Delivery Type (if you haven't already):
  • Delivery Type: Create a new delivery type (e.g., "ZIN")
  • Configuration:
    • Go to IMG -> Logistics Execution -> Shipping -> Deliveries -> Define Delivery Types
    • Create a new entry for your custom delivery type
    • Define the relevant order type (e.g., DL for deliveries not linked to sales orders)
    • Assign the appropriate confirmation control key 
       
3. Assign the Custom Inbound Delivery Type to the Custom STO Type:
  • Configuration:
    • Go to IMG -> Material Management -> Purchasing -> Confirmations -> Set Up Confirmation Control
    • Create a new confirmation control key (e.g., "Z004")
    • Ensure the "GR-Relevant" and "GR Assignment Key" checkboxes are selected
    • Assign your custom inbound delivery type (e.g., "ZIN") to the confirmation control key
    • Assign your custom STO type (e.g., "ZUB") to the confirmation control key 
       
 
4. Create STO and Inbound Delivery:
  • Create STO:
    • Use transaction ME21N
    • Select your custom STO type (e.g., "ZUB")
    • Maintain the relevant data (material, quantity, etc.)
    • Save the STO
  • Create Inbound Delivery:
    • Use transaction VL31N
    • Enter the vendor and purchase order number
    • The system should automatically recognize your custom inbound delivery type based on the STO type and confirmation control settings
    • Save the inbound delivery
  • Post Goods Receipt:
    • Use transaction VL32N (Change Inbound Delivery) or VL31N (Create Inbound Delivery)
    • Post the goods receipt for the inbound delivery 

    Hope it's clarifies your query.

    Regards,

    Neeraj Jain

entry.title: Re: Quantity and Alternative Quantity in Transportation C...
entry.content:
Good afternoon,
My name is Rakell Souza.
I am from Brazil.
Nice to meet you!
I will try to help you.

It looks like you are facing an issue in SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM), specifically in the Transportation Cockpit (TC), where the Quantity and Alternative Quantity fields are empty.

Suggestions to solve the issue:

  1. Check if the data is being correctly populated:

    • Ensure that the Freight Units (FU) or Freight Orders (FO) have assigned quantities.

    • In SAP TM, quantities usually come from Sales Orders (SO) or Deliveries (DTR - Delivery-based Transportation Requirements).

  2. Check the Planning Profile Settings:

    • In the Transportation Cockpit, click on Change Profile Selection → Verify if the quantity display is enabled in the profile settings.

    • Try switching the planning profile to another one that may display the quantities.

  3. Validate Master Data and Documents:

    • Ensure that the products are correctly configured with Units of Measure (UoM) and that the quantities are set in the Document Flow.

    • Verify if the Material Master Data has the correct unit of measure for transportation.

  4. Check the Transport Document Data:

    • Go to the corresponding Freight Unit or Freight Order and verify if the quantities are registered there.

  5. Display Hidden Columns:

    • Click on Display Settings and check if the Quantity and Alternative Quantity columns are hidden.

  6. Technical Validation (BOPF Debugging):

    • If the data actually exists in the Freight Unit or Freight Order but does not appear in the cockpit, debugging in BOPF might be necessary to check the data structure.

If none of these options solve the issue, it could be a SAP TM configuration problem or even a technical error that requires SAP support. 🚀

entry.title: Re: SAC – Integrated Financial Planning – Data model and...
entry.content:
Hello,

Let me address your questions. 

1) At a high-level, in Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA, the balance sheet is planned using a combination of user input and data actions.  There are 4 methods that can be assigned to each balance sheet account using the “Calc Method” property in the SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT dimension.

               CHANGING_INPUT: User enters the changing amount and the data action calculates the opening & closing amounts

               CLOSING_INPUT:  User enters the closing amount and the data action calculates the opening & changing amounts

               DRIVER_INPUT:  User doesn’t directly plan the balance sheet account but instead enters a driver such as Days Sales Outstanding or FTE.  A data action calculates the opening, changing & closing amounts for the associated balance sheet account.  I will discuss the details further down.

               CHANGING_CALC:  The data action calculates these balance sheet accounts directly.  One example is accumulated depreciation where the data action will use the depreciation planned in the P&L section as the input.  Another example is the cash account which is used to as the plug amount to balance the balance sheet.

At the end of the process, each balance sheet account, regardless of the planning method, will have an opening, changing and closing amount for each Plan period.

Your assumption regarding which accounts are planned by closing_input and which by changing_input is not entirely correct.  It is the customer’s choice on which method to use based on their business processes.  Certain accounts such as debt may be convenient to plan by closing amount while others such as PP&E may be convenient to plan by changing amount.  But by updating the “Calc Method” dimension property, you can customize it as desired.  You can also choose to exclusively use one method.

2) The second tab of the story SAP_FI_IFP_IM_FinancialStatement_Planning demonstrates driver-based planning.  The example delivered is using days sales outstanding (DSO) to plan Accounts Receivables and days payables outstanding (DPO) to plan Accounts Payable.

There are four steps:

  • Run the data action “Calculate Drivers”.  Based on last year’s actuals (e.g. Revenue, Accounts Receivable), the data action calculates historical DSO/DPO to use as a starting point
  • User adjusts the DSO/DPO amounts as desired
  • Run the data action “Calculate Closing Amounts”.  Based on the DSO/DPO drivers and previously inputted P&L amounts for plan (e.g. revenue, COGS), the data action calculates the closing amounts for the Accounts Receivable/Accounts Payable balance sheet accounts.
  • Run the data action “Calculate Balance Sheet”.  Opening & changing amounts are calculated based on the closing amounts

A customer could expand this section to use other drivers, e.g. using headcount to plan buildings, but they would be required to update the data actions with the necessary calculations.

 

3) I believe this is answered above.  Basically, customize to your needs by selecting either Changing_Input or Closing_Input.  Driver_Input can be used with the understanding of the necessary updates to the data actions.  Any balance sheet account with an empty Calc_Method is excluded from planning.

4) The fifth tab of the story SAP_FI_IFP_IM_FinancialStatement_Planning (Balance Sheet Report) or the third tab of the story SAP_FI_IFP_IM_FinancialStatement_Reports (View Balance Sheet) should be used to view the balance sheet in its entirety.  After running the data action “Calculate Balance Sheet”, the end result of all four balance sheet planning methods is available.  The other tabs in  SAP_FI_IFP_IM_FinancialStatement_Planning should be viewed as for data input only.

Best regards.

entry.title: Three new supply chain logistics customer stories
entry.content:
Q1 brought three customer stories of supply chain logistics transformation.  

Here's to our valued customers, and the dedicated teams who bring value to their customers.

Celsa UK: Enabling closer collaboration with carriers to create a future-ready supply chain network

"When steel producer Celsa UK looked to improve cost-efficiency and visibility across its logistics network, it realized that effective collaboration with its carriers would be key to success. To help it achieve this, the company implemented SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, transportation management and the SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration solution."   (Celsa webinar)

✔️1-2% savings on transport costs | ✔️95% of road carriers collaborating through the network | ✔️83% on-time deliveries

Bondalti: Transforming logistics processes to better manage transportation, yard operations, and carrier collaboration

"A leading specialty chemicals and solutions company, Bondalti sought to reimagine its logistics processes. The SAP Transportation Management and SAP Yard Logistics applications as well as the SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration solution provide improved visibility and planning in transportation while integrating carriers for freight management and supply chain transparency."

✔️4% average reduction in transportation costs | ✔️90% of carrier invoices received digitally, compliant, and error-free | ✔️25% less time spent by trucks in the plants

LAPP: Keeping industries moving by delivering certainty in an unpredictable world with enhanced supply chain visibility

"Many of LAPP’s customers are manufacturing companies that operate on tight production schedules, so any delays in shipping can cause significant disruption. To give its customers more certainty about delivery dates, LAPP wanted to boost transparency and efficiency in its inbound supply chain through smarter collaboration with suppliers and carriers."

✔️90%+ faster automated confirmation of purchase orders | ✔️30%+ time savings on order management and delivery tracking for inbound deliveries | ✔️10% productivity gains for customer service and logistics teams

 

Learn more about #LogisticsVisibility: 
SAP Community posts
SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace: product tour
Coming soon: Supply Chain Logistics maturity model for self-assessment and business case

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything FEATURING Kafka
entry.content:
Digital transformation requires powerful and flexible integration solutions to efficiently connect disparate systems. The SAP Integration Suite provides comprehensive capabilities for linking various applications and data sources.

One technology for processing large volumes of data in real time is Apache Kafka. It is a distributed event-streaming platform known for its scalability and reliability. The combination of SAP Integration Suite and Kafka enables seamless business processes with real-time data streams. This article explores three key setups for Kafka integration and is the third part in a series dedicated to exploring how SAP Integration Suite is enabling organizations to integrate everything

  1. SAP Advanced Event Mesh as a Kafka Sender

  2. SAP Advanced Event Mesh as a Kafka Receiver

  3. SAP Integration Suite Kafka Adapter

The following image illustrates the connections between the components:

Setup 1: SAP Advanced Event Mesh as a Kafka Sender

Overview: The SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) enables real-time event processing within an enterprise network. In this setup, it acts as a Kafka Sender, forwarding messages to a Kafka cluster.

Use Case: An SAP system sends data to the Advanced Event Mesh, which should be published/forwarded to Kafka.

 

Create Kafka API Key

Create a Kafka API Key which will provide you access credential, there access Kafka -> access your Cluster > API Keys > Add Key.  

You already can add the correct Access Control List (ACL) you would like to use, we will do it in later step and continue with the API Key creation first, in order to establish the technical connection first. Once we have a stable connection we will configure proper access rules using ACL. Once API Key and Secret have been created, save the credentials, as you do not have access to the secret anymore. We will use them in SAP AEM to connect.

 

Setup Connection in SAP AEM : Define connection parameters to the Kafka cluster

Access the Broker you would like to use for your Kafka bridge. In the SAP AEM Cloud console navigate to Manage > Bridges

Navigate forward via Kafka Bridges >Kafka Senders> Add Kafka Sender.

Enable the Sender and ensure that you add the minimum required parameter like Bootstrap address ( you get it from your cluster), user and Secret. Click Apply and check if the connection has been established.

In case your setup was correct you will see the Admin Status and Operational Status as "Up".

Congratulations - you have established the technical connection, let's continue with the Queue Binding.  

Queue & Topic Binding

In this step we assign the Queue to the Kafka sender which will be used to forward the events and publish them to the Remote (Kafka) Topic. Besides these minimum requirements you have the option of further advanced settings, which we skip in this blog. Therefore I have created a topic (with 6 Partitions)in Kafka "WelcomeSAPEvents" which we will use.

Once you saved it you will get an error "Broker: Topic authorization failed" Why? We still need to allow that our API Key is allowed to write into that topic. Remember the very first step API Key creation. Therefore navigate in your Kafka Cluster to your API Key > Access > Topic > Edit Topic. Add our Topic "WelcomeSAPEvents" and give WRITE permission ALLOW. 

 
You have completed the SAP AEM Kafka Sender setup. Use the TryMe feature to perform a test. Let's continue in the next section with the Kafka Receiver setup.
 

Setup 2: SAP Advanced Event Mesh as a Kafka Receiver

Overview: Here, the Advanced Event Mesh receives messages from Kafka and forwards them to connected systems.

Use Case: A Kafka topic containing customer orders is subscribed to by AEM. The order data is then transferred to SAP S/4HANA or other applications.

Setup Connection in SAP AEM: Define connection parameters to the Kafka cluster

For our setup we are using the APIKey and Topic created in Setup 1.

The very first step is the Kafka Receiver Setup, therefore access the Broker you would like to use for your Kafka bridge. In the SAP AEM Cloud console navigate to Manage > Bridges> > Kafka Receiver> Add Kafka Receiver.

Enable the Receiver and ensure that you add the minimum required parameter like Bootstrap address ( you get it from your cluster), user and Secret. Click Apply and check if the connection has been established.

 Once done you will see, that the technical connect (Admin Status) has been established, but the Operational Status is display as "Down", with the error description "No remote broker in UP state". To receiver the data you have to set the right remote (Kafka) topics which you would like to subscribe.

 

Topic Binding

Navigate to Manage > Bridges> > Kafka Receiver> Choose your receiver > Topic Binding > Add Topic Binding. Maintain the parameters, in my case the topic name is "WelcomeSAPEvents". Keep in mind this is the topic name from Kafka.

Inside the topic subscription binding,  we have to maintain an internal (local) topic which will be used SAP AEM internally, in my case the topic name is "SAPAEMInternalEvent".

 

Queue Binding

In order to track the data, let's create a queue to store the data. Navigate to Queue > Create Queue.

Once done, also add the corresponding subscription to your setup.Navigate to Queue > Subscription > AddSubscription. I added the subscription forS APAEMInternalEvent.

 

Maintain correct access rights (consumer group)

The very last step is now to maintain the correct ConsumerGroupID on SAP AEM, but also in Kafka. But where should we maintain it? Let's have a look.

In SAP AEM: Navigate to Manage > Bridges> > Kafka Receiver> Choose your receiver > Settings > Advanced Settings >Section Group and maintain a group. For our setup I have decided for "test".

 

The same ConsumerGroupID we have to maintain in Kafka. Concrete we have to maintain the value within the Kafka API Key Security section. In Kafka : Navigate to Cluster Overview > API Key > Select your API > Access > Consumer group.

 

 

Congratulations, you have established the Kafka Receiver bridge in SAP Advanced Event Mesh. The Operational status of the bridge changed from "Down" to "Up". You can use the common an known monitoring tools of both applications to track the data/event exchange. In my case I have created a queue which has subscribed to the Kafka topic, therefore I simply can check the messages in this queue.

Setup 3: SAP Integration Suite Kafka Adapter

 

SAP Cloud Integration provides a Kafka Adapter that enables direct message exchange between SAP applications and Kafka clusters. This adapter supports both sending and receiving Kafka messages.

Use Case

When businesses require a direct integration between SAP Cloud Integration and Kafka for structured data exchange, this setup provides a flexible and scalable solution.

 

Key Steps

  • Configure the Kafka Adapter in SAP Cloud Integration.

  • Set up authentication and connectivity to the Kafka cluster.

  • Define integration flows to send or consume messages via Kafka topics.

  • Monitor and manage message processing through SAP Cloud Integration.

As there are already so many good blogs out there, i decided to link them, instead of repeating the setup step here again. Kindly check the blog from @ChrisQi  "Build Your First Scenario with SAP Cloud Integration Kafka Adapter" or the blog from @jens_kordowski "Cloud Integration - What You Need to Know About the Kafka Adapter".

 

Conclusion

The combination of SAP Integration Suite and Kafka opens up powerful possibilities for real-time data processing. Whether as a sender, receiver, or via the Kafka Adapter – each setup offers specific advantages and can be flexibly deployed depending on requirements.

The SAP Integration Suite remains a cornerstone of modern, event-driven architectures, enabling businesses to seamlessly connect various systems like Kafka as one event-streaming platform.

What are your experiences with Kafka integration in SAP? Share your thoughts and use cases in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Fieldglass - Multiple Item for Workorder and Integrat...
entry.content:
Hello! Answering some of the questions below I was able to gather for you. For your specific instance it would be good to explore this via a case as there could be some nuance based on your specific situation.

  1. Can we create multiple lines on one Workorder ? If yes , does the integration between FG and Ariba will create one PO for Multiple WO Lines or One PO per WO line ?
    Typically where we see this is where the Spend Matrix functionality is being used within SAP Fieldglass that can allow multi-line PO support is added for contingent workflow. On detection of the Work Order utilizing the Spend Matrix, a multi-line payload will be created and sent to Ariba Buying.
  2. Can we create multiple lines on one SOW  ? If yes, does the integration between FG and Ariba will create one PO for Multiple  SOW Lines or One PO per SOW  line ?
    In SAP Fieldglass the company configuration setting Ariba Integration for SOW with multi-line allows you to send PO requisitions to SAP Ariba P2P with multiple line-items. This setting must be enabled to send POs to SAP Ariba with multiple line-items.
  3. For multiple Cost Center for same WO line or SOW Line in Fieldglass, does the integration with Ariba will split the Ariba PO  with the same splitting in FG WO or FG SOW  ? Or does the WO or SOW integration in FG/Ariba is limited to only the Primary Cost Center (flag in FG) to be replicated in Ariba PO ?
    The scenarios that can be cared for include SOW Multi-Line PO – Single Cost Object, SOW Multi-Line PO – Multiple Cost Objects, Contingent Single Line PO – Multiple Cost Objects, Contingent Spend Matrix – Multi-Line PO’s. One thing to note here, is unlike the single contingent payloads, each line on a multi-line contingent payload will only relate to one cost object with a 100% cost allocation.

entry.title: Re: Production order /Process order settlement Public cloud
entry.content:
SAP Public Cloud offers 2 options to proces/production orders' settlement but the 2nd option (BEI: traditional month end activities)is deprecated (SAP will likely to lift this option's support). 

 The 1st option is the preferred one which is under 3FO Scope which also offers 2 options:

1) WIP or variance is calculated & posted as per the status (partially delivered/fully delivered) of order on goods movements & confirmations)

Pros and Cons:

No need of manual calculations of WIP & Variances & settlement at month end but the drawback is that a document is posted at every goods movement & confirmation which results in too many documents that doesn't suit where the orders' life is very short.

2) Schedule batch jobs to manually post at month end.

Pros & Cons:

A single document is posted as per the schedule job but this requires a manual effort at the month end.

Regards,

Muhammad Humayun Khan

entry.title: Required Guidance to Expose REST API Externally
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,
We need to create and host two secure APIs in SAP to integrate with Bank for authentication and transaction processing. These APIs will facilitate automated authentication and transaction status updates between SAP and Bank, ensuring seamless, secure, and efficient communication.

Business Requirement:

Bank requires two endpoints:

Authentication API – Bank will send an authentication request to SAP, which should validate the credentials and return an authentication token. This token will then be used for further API interactions, eliminating the need for manual username/password entry.

Late Return Payment Status API – Bank will send encrypted status updates for late return payments via HTTPS. SAP must decrypt the request, validate the data, update the transaction status, and send a response.

These APIs must be externally accessible, enforce strong security mechanisms, and comply with banking and SAP security standards.

Technical Requirements:

Authentication Mechanism: The API should support automatic authentication and prevent unauthorized access. SBI should be able to call the authentication URL securely without requiring manual login like API URL.

Encryption & Security: Data should be encrypted in transit and at rest. Authentication and late return status updates should be processed securely using encryption, signature verification, and request integrity validation.

External Hosting Considerations: The URLs must be accessible externally with proper security controls.

Token Management: The authentication API should generate a secure token that SBI can use for further API requests to SAP.

Logging & Monitoring: All incoming API requests should be logged for audit purposes, and SAP should monitor API activity to detect anomalies.

Authentication API Flow:

BANK→ [HTTPS Request] → SAP (Authentication URL)

SAP → [Validate Credentials] → Internal Authentication Logic

SAP → [Generate Auth Token] → Internal Token Storage

SAP → [HTTPS Response with Token] →BANK

BANK → [Use Token for API Calls] → SAP Transactions

Late Return Payment Status API Flow:

BANK→ [HTTPS Request with Encrypted Data] → SAP (Late Return API URL)

SAP → [Decrypt Request] → Internal Processing

SAP → [Validate & Update Payment Status] → SAP Database

SAP → [HTTPS Response: Status Updated] →BANK

We need to securely expose two REST-based APIs in SAP for integration with SBI Bank using JSON over HTTPS. These APIs will handle authentication and transaction status updates.

  1. Authentication API – Bank calls this API for authentication, and SAP responds with an authentication token.
  1. Late Return Status API – Bank calls this API with encrypted late return payment status updates, which SAP decrypts, processes, and acknowledges.

Technical Details:

Data Format: JSON

Protocol: REST (HTTPS)

Authentication: Client certificate authentication (mutual TLS) and token-based authentication

Security: TLS 1.2/1.3, digital signature verification, IP whitelisting

SSL Certificate: Signed by a trusted CA (not self-signed)

Hosting: SAP should securely expose the APIs externally for Bank access

Request for Support:

1.What are the recommended SAP-supported approaches for hosting secure REST-based APIs?

2.Which SAP authentication mechanisms align best with mutual TLS and token-based authentication?

3.What SAP components and configurations are recommended for secure REST API exposure?

Looking forward to your expert guidance.

entry.title: SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 + ESM - Token Based Authentication (Avoid BasicAuth protocol)
entry.content:
Token-based authentication is essential for several reasons, especially in today's technology with SAP BTP Integrations, SAP Build for web and mobile applications . Here are some key benefits:

  1. Security: Tokens are more secure than traditional session-based authentication. They are typically short-lived and can be easily revoked if compromised. This reduces the risk of unauthorized access.

  2. Scalability: Token-based authentication is stateless, meaning the server does not need to store session information. This makes it easier to scale applications horizontally, as each request is self-contained.

  3. Flexibility: Tokens can be used across different domains and services, enabling single sign-on (SSO) and integration with third-party services. This is particularly useful in microservices architectures.

  4. Performance: Since tokens are self-contained and do not require server-side session storage, they can improve the performance of your application by reducing the load on the server.

  5. User Experience: Tokens can be stored on the client side (e.g., in local storage or cookies), allowing users to remain authenticated across multiple sessions and devices without needing to log in repeatedly.

  6. SAP Build Apps, Side by Side Extensions and API Integration: Token-based authentication is well-suited for various business use cases for designing extensions for web, mobile applications and APIs, where maintaining server-side sessions is impractical. Tokens can be easily included in HTTP headers for secure communication

Note : Currently only REST is supported.  No Odata APIs are existed today

SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 - API Documentation 

Below template is ready usable for Visual Studio Code with Rest Client Extension enabled. Just copy and create as file as api.http

@URL = https://XXXXXX.de1.demo.crm.cloud.sap
@user = XXXXXXXXX
@password = XXXXXXXXXX



###
#  login
GET {{url}}/sap/c4c/api/v1/iam-service/token
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Basic {{user}}:{{password}}

@accessToken = {{login.response.body.value.access_token}}

###
GET {{url}}/auth/token?grant_type=refresh_token
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}


###
GET {{url}}/sap/c4c/api/v1/iam-service/users?$top=15&$skip=0
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}

Kindly share the blog to all your colleagues who can get benefited.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, how do you use "Create vers...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Group Reporting, when you create a new version using the "Create Version with Template" functionality, the system locks the version once it is created. This means:

  1. No Deletion – You cannot delete the version once it has been created.
  2. No Modifications – You cannot change the values entered during the creation process.
  3. Version Management – While you cannot undo a created version, you can still create additional versions if needed.

This does not mean that you can only use one version. You can create multiple versions for different scenarios (e.g., Actuals, Forecasts, Budgets). However, each version is fixed once created, ensuring data consistency for financial consolidation.

For financial consolidation, this means that once a version is generated and used in reports, it remains as a reference point. If adjustments are needed, you would have to create a new version instead of modifying the existing one. This approach ensures auditability and compliance with financial reporting standards.

I hope this will help you answer your questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

The initial dataset is filtered by the RESULTLOOKUP, and while querying the output, I’ve noticed that the records are aggregated at the account level, but not at the Cost Centre level, date etc. Specifically, I see three different accounts getting aggregated, yet we did not specify that account = # in the data keyword. On the other hand, for the cost centre, there are two distinct values (17101750 and 17101753) which are not getting aggregated.

entry.title: Re: Consumption in bill of material
entry.content: Use an Alternate Unit of Measure for the component: PAA ('Pair') or similar, without decimals so that the system rounds to whole units. Assign it to the component with 1 PAA = 2 PC and set it as Unit of Issue. Then maintain the BOM item with quantity 1 PAA (with regard to the 10 PC Base quantity of the BOM).  Your requirements will be rounded up to Pairs.

entry.title: Re: How to check and display my own cases in SAP for Me -...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

You can use the filter feature in the Case List: select the case status (select all status, or some of the status), select your S-user as the Reporter, or the other filters, e.g. priority, system number, creation date, etc.

Then click Go to display your own cases.

Then go to Personal view and save your settings/filters.

More information about how to use the Case list, kindly refer to KBA 2931236 - How to work with the newly designed Case List - SAP for Me.

Hope the above information helps.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Debug Application Job on S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Juwin ,

it's fine in development/customizing tenant but in quality or production system it doesn't run.

Popup with parameter doesn't appear even debugger is activated for tenant.

Is it forbidden to debug job in production tenant?

Best regards

Giuseppe

entry.title: Is it possible to use F4 help event of CL_GUI_ALV_GRID for CL_SALV_TABLE related program
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a program where I want to create custom F4 help depending on the line selected. Please find the code below requesting you to please let me know if there is any solution. I have tried to change the field catalog for two fields to make them editable, but that's not working. Please suggest a correction for this, too.

@Sandra_Rossi 

@naimesh_patel 

@former_member182550 

@raymond_giuseppi 

 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZPPMAT_QRPRINBT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT ZPPMAT_QRPRINBT.
TABLES : MSEG,MKPF,MARA,MAKT.
SELECTION-SCREEN : BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-H01.
  SELECT-OPTIONS: S_MBLNR FOR MSEG-MBLNR.
SELECTION-SCREEN : END OF BLOCK B1.

CLASS LCL_EVENT_HANDLER DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.


    METHODS:
      ON_LINK_CLICK
        FOR EVENT
        LINK_CLICK OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE
        IMPORTING ROW COLUMN.
    METHODS ON_ADDED_FUNCTION
      FOR EVENT IF_SALV_EVENTS_FUNCTIONS~ADDED_FUNCTION OF CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE
      IMPORTING E_SALV_FUNCTION..


ENDCLASS.                    "lcl_event_handler DEFINITION

CLASS LCL_REPORT DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION .
*   ALV reference
    INTERFACES IF_ALV_RM_GRID_FRIEND .
    DATA: LO_FUNCTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_FUNCTIONS_LIST.
    DATA: LO_SELECTIONS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_SELECTIONS.
    DATA: LO_SALV TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_TABLE.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_MKPF,
             MBLNR TYPE MBLNR,
             MJAHR TYPE MJAHR,
             BLDAT TYPE BLDAT,
             BUDAT TYPE BUDAT,
             XBLNR TYPE XBLNR,
           END OF TY_MKPF.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF TY_MSEG,
             MBLNR      TYPE MBLNR,
             MJAHR      TYPE MJAHR,
             WERKS      TYPE MSEG-WERKS,
             MATNR      TYPE MATNR,
             CHARG      TYPE CHARG_D,
             LIFNR      TYPE LIFNR,
             MENGE      TYPE MENGE_D,
             MEINS      TYPE MEINS,
             EBELN      TYPE EBELN,
             ABLAD      TYPE ABLAD,
             VFDAT      TYPE VFDAT,
             HSDAT      TYPE HSDAT,
             BUDAT_MKPF TYPE BUDAT,
           END OF TY_MSEG.

    TYPES:BEGIN OF TY_FINAL,
            CHECK       TYPE YQRSTR-ZCHECK,
            MBLNR       TYPE YQRSTR-MBLNR,
            MJAHR       TYPE MSEG-MJAHR,
            WERKS       TYPE MSEG-WERKS,
            MATNR       TYPE YQRSTR-MATNR,
            MAKTX       TYPE YQRSTR-MAKTX,
            MENGE       TYPE YQRSTR-MENGE,
            MEINS       TYPE YQRSTR-MEINS,
            SKUN        TYPE YQRSTR-SKUN,
            QUAN        TYPE CHAR10,
            CREATED_BY  TYPE NAME1,
            VERIFIED_BY TYPE NAME1,
            BOX_UNIT    TYPE STRING,
          END OF TY_FINAL.

    DATA :IT_FINAL TYPE TABLE OF TY_FINAL,
          WA_FINAL TYPE  TY_FINAL.
    DATA: IT_MSEG    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_MSEG,
          LO_COLUMNS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMNS,
          LO_COLUMN  TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_COLUMN_TABLE,
          LO_EVENT   TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE.


    TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_MARA,
              MATNR TYPE MARA-MATNR,
              FERTH TYPE MARA-FERTH,
            END OF TY_MARA.
    TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_MAKT,
              MATNR TYPE MAKT-MATNR,
              MAKTX TYPE MAKT-MAKTX,
              SPRAS TYPE MAKT-SPRAS,
            END OF TY_MAKT.

    DATA: IT_MKPF  TYPE TABLE OF  TY_MKPF,
          WA_MKPF  TYPE  TY_MKPF,
          IT_MSEG1 TYPE TABLE OF  TY_MSEG,
          WA_MSEG1 TYPE  TY_MSEG.
    DATA: IT_MARA TYPE TABLE OF TY_MARA,
          IT_MAKT TYPE TABLE OF TY_MAKT.

    DATA:
      LS_KEY    TYPE SALV_S_LAYOUT_KEY,
      LO_LAYOUT TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_LAYOUT.
    METHODS:
      HANDLE_ON_F4
        FOR EVENT ONF4 OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID
        IMPORTING E_FIELDNAME
                  ES_ROW_NO
                  ER_EVENT_DATA.
    METHODS:
      DOUBLE_CLICK FOR EVENT DOUBLE_CLICK OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID IMPORTING E_ROW
                                                                       E_COLUMN
                                                                       ES_ROW_NO.
    METHODS: EVH_AFTER_REFRESH FOR EVENT AFTER_REFRESH OF CL_GUI_ALV_GRID IMPORTING SENDER.
    METHODS : GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA,
      DISPLAY.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA: HANDLER_ADDED TYPE ABAP_BOOL.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS LCL_REPORT IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA .
    SELECT  MBLNR MJAHR BLDAT BUDAT XBLNR FROM MKPF
          INTO TABLE IT_MKPF
          WHERE MBLNR IN S_MBLNR.

    IF IT_MKPF[] IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT MBLNR
             MJAHR
             WERKS
             MATNR
             CHARG
             LIFNR
             MENGE
             MEINS
             EBELN
             ABLAD
             VFDAT
             HSDAT
             BUDAT_MKPF
        FROM MSEG
        INTO TABLE IT_MSEG
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MKPF
        WHERE MBLNR = IT_MKPF-MBLNR AND
              BWART = '101'.
    ENDIF.
    IF IT_MSEG IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT MATNR
             FERTH  FROM MARA INTO TABLE IT_MARA
             FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MSEG WHERE MATNR = IT_MSEG-MATNR ." SKU Value

      SELECT MATNR
             MAKTX
             SPRAS FROM MAKT INTO TABLE IT_MAKT
        FOR ALL ENTRIES IN IT_MSEG WHERE MATNR = IT_MSEG-MATNR
                                     AND SPRAS = SY-LANGU .

    ENDIF.


    LOOP AT IT_MSEG INTO DATA(WA_MSEG).
      WA_FINAL-MBLNR = WA_MSEG-MBLNR.
      WA_FINAL-MJAHR = WA_MSEG-MJAHR.
      WA_FINAL-WERKS = WA_MSEG-WERKS.
      READ TABLE IT_MARA INTO DATA(WA_MARA) WITH KEY MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR.
      IF SY-SUBRC = 0 .
        WA_FINAL-MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR.
        READ TABLE IT_makt INTO DATA(WA_MAKT) WITH KEY MATNR = WA_MSEG-MATNR .
        IF  SY-SUBRC = 0 .
          WA_FINAL-MAKTX = WA_MAKT-MAKTX.
        ENDIF.
        WA_FINAL-MENGE = WA_MSEG-MENGE.
        WA_FINAL-MEINS = WA_MSEG-MEINS.
        READ TABLE IT_MARA INTO WA_MARA WITH KEY MATNR = WA_FINAL-MATNR .
        IF SY-SUBRC = 0 .
          WA_FINAL-SKUN = WA_MARA-FERTH.
          IF WA_FINAL-SKUN IS NOT INITIAL .
            WA_FINAL-QUAN = WA_FINAL-MENGE / WA_FINAL-SKUN.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
      APPEND WA_FINAL TO IT_FINAL.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD .
  METHOD DISPLAY.
    TRY.
        CALL METHOD CL_SALV_TABLE=>FACTORY
          IMPORTING
            R_SALV_TABLE = LO_SALV
          CHANGING
            T_TABLE      = IT_FINAL[].

        IF lO_SALV IS BOUND .
          CALL METHOD LO_SALV->SET_SCREEN_STATUS
            EXPORTING
              REPORT        = SY-REPID
              PFSTATUS      = 'ZSTANDARD' "your PF Status name
              SET_FUNCTIONS = LO_SALV->C_FUNCTIONS_ALL.


          LS_KEY-REPORT = SY-REPID.
          LO_LAYOUT = LO_SALV->GET_LAYOUT( ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_SAVE_RESTRICTION( IF_SALV_C_LAYOUT=>RESTRICT_NONE ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_KEY( LS_KEY ).
          LO_LAYOUT->SET_DEFAULT( ABAP_TRUE ).

          LO_COLUMNS = LO_SALV->GET_COLUMNS( ).
          LO_COLUMNS->SET_OPTIMIZE( 'X' ).
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'CHECK' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Check' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_CELL_TYPE( IF_SALV_C_CELL_TYPE=>CHECKBOX_HOTSPOT ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'QUAN' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Bag Quantity' ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'CREATED_BY' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Inspected By' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'VERIFIED_BY' ). " Find the 'MAKTX' column ans change attributes
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Verified By' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          TRY.
              LO_COLUMN ?= LO_COLUMNS->GET_COLUMN( 'BOX_UNIT' ). " Find the 'MAKTX' column ans change attributes
              LO_COLUMN->SET_VISIBLE( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_LONG_TEXT( 'Box Unit' ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_F4(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
              LO_COLUMN->SET_OPTIMIZED(
                VALUE = IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE
              ).
            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND.
            CATCH CX_SALV_EXISTING.
            CATCH CX_SALV_DATA_ERROR.
          ENDTRY.
          DATA LO_SETTINGS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_DISPLAY_SETTINGS.
          LO_SETTINGS = LO_SALV->GET_DISPLAY_SETTINGS( ).
          LO_SETTINGS->SET_STRIPED_PATTERN( IF_SALV_C_BOOL_SAP=>TRUE ).

          DATA: LO_EVENTS TYPE REF TO CL_SALV_EVENTS_TABLE,
                G_GRID    TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
          LO_EVENTS = LO_SALV->GET_EVENT( ).

          DATA: LO_EVENT_HANDLER TYPE REF TO LCL_EVENT_HANDLER.
          CREATE OBJECT LO_EVENT_HANDLER.

          SET HANDLER HANDLE_ON_F4 FOR ALL INSTANCES.
          SET HANDLER DOUBLE_CLICK FOR ALL INSTANCES.
          DATA(SELECTIONS) = LO_SALV->GET_SELECTIONS( ).
          SELECTIONS->SET_SELECTION_MODE(   IF_SALV_C_SELECTION_MODE=>CELL  ). "Single row selection
          SET HANDLER LO_EVENT_HANDLER->ON_LINK_CLICK FOR LO_EVENTS.
          SET HANDLER LO_EVENT_HANDLER->ON_ADDED_FUNCTION FOR LO_EVENTS.

*          DATA :MO_EDIT  TYPE REF TO IF_SALV_GUI_OM_EDIT_RESTRICTED.
*          MO_EDIT = LO_SALV->EXTENDED_GRID_API( )->EDITABLE_RESTRICTED( ).
*
*          TRY.
*              MO_EDIT->SET_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_COLUMNNAME(
*                EXPORTING
*                  COLUMNNAME              = 'VERIFIED_BY'
*                  ALL_CELLS_INPUT_ENABLED = ABAP_TRUE
*              ).
*            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND. " ALV: General Error Class (Checked in Syntax Check)
*          ENDTRY.
*          TRY.
*              MO_EDIT->SET_ATTRIBUTES_FOR_COLUMNNAME(
*                EXPORTING
*                  COLUMNNAME              = 'CREATED_BY'
*                  ALL_CELLS_INPUT_ENABLED = ABAP_TRUE
*              ).
*            CATCH CX_SALV_NOT_FOUND. " ALV: General Error Class (Checked in Syntax Check)
*          ENDTRY.
          LO_SALV->DISPLAY( ).
        ENDIF .
      CATCH CX_ROOT .

    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD HANDLE_ON_F4.
    BREAK-POINT.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD DOUBLE_CLICK.
    BREAK-POINT.
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD EVH_AFTER_REFRESH.
    CHECK HANDLER_ADDED EQ ABAP_FALSE.
*    SET HANDLER ME->EVH_DEL_CHANGE_SELECTION FOR SENDER.

*    "to use this method you need to inherit from cl_gui_alv_grid or add interface IF_ALV_RM_GRID_FRIEND
*    "to your class
    SENDER->SET_DELAY_CHANGE_SELECTION(
      EXPORTING
        TIME   = 100    " Time in Milliseconds
      EXCEPTIONS
        ERROR  = 1
        OTHERS = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->REGISTER_DELAYED_EVENT(
      EXPORTING
        I_EVENT_ID = SENDER->MC_EVT_DELAYED_CHANGE_SELECT
      EXCEPTIONS
        ERROR      = 1
        OTHERS     = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->GET_FRONTEND_FIELDCATALOG(
      IMPORTING
        ET_FIELDCATALOG = DATA(FCAT)    " Field Catalog
    ).

    "setting editable fields
    ASSIGN FCAT[ FIELDNAME = 'VERIFIED_BY' ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<FCAT>).
    IF SY-SUBRC EQ 0.
      <FCAT>-EDIT = ABAP_TRUE.
    ENDIF.

    ASSIGN FCAT[ FIELDNAME = 'CREATED_BY' ] TO <FCAT>.
    IF SY-SUBRC EQ 0.
      <FCAT>-EDIT = ABAP_TRUE.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->SET_FRONTEND_FIELDCATALOG( IT_FIELDCATALOG = FCAT ).
    SENDER->REGISTER_EDIT_EVENT(
      EXPORTING
        I_EVENT_ID = SENDER->MC_EVT_MODIFIED    " Event ID
*      exceptions
*       error      = 1
*       others     = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

    SENDER->SET_READY_FOR_INPUT(
      I_READY_FOR_INPUT = 1
    ).

    HANDLER_ADDED = ABAP_TRUE.
    SENDER->REFRESH_TABLE_DISPLAY(
*      exporting
*        is_stable      =     " With Stable Rows/Columns
*        i_soft_refresh =     " Without Sort, Filter, etc.
*      exceptions
*        finished       = 1
*        others         = 2
    ).
    IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
*     message id sy-msgid type sy-msgty number sy-msgno
*                with sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.


  ENDMETHOD.


ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA: LO_REPORT TYPE REF TO LCL_REPORT.
  CREATE OBJECT LO_REPORT.
  LO_REPORT->GET_DATA_AND_FILL_DATA( ).
  LO_REPORT->DISPLAY( ).

CLASS LCL_EVENT_HANDLER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD ON_LINK_CLICK.
    DATA :MO_EDIT  TYPE REF TO IF_SALV_GUI_OM_EDIT_RESTRICTED.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <LFA_DATA> LIKE LINE OF LO_REPORT->IT_FINAl.
    READ TABLE LO_REPORT->IT_FINAL ASSIGNING <LFA_DATA> INDEX ROW.
    CHECK SY-SUBRC IS INITIAL.
    IF <LFA_DATA>-CHECK IS INITIAL.
      <LFA_DATA>-CHECK = 'X'.
    ELSE.
      CLEAR <LFA_DATA>-CHECK.
    ENDIF.
    LO_REPORT->LO_SALV->REFRESH( ).
  ENDMETHOD.                    "on_link_click
  METHOD ON_ADDED_FUNCTION.
    DATA: IT_BAPIRET2 TYPE TABLE OF BAPIRET2,
          WA_BAPIRET2 TYPE BAPIRET2.

    DATA: LO_GRID TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID.
    IF LO_GRID IS INITIAL.
      CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'
        IMPORTING
          E_GRID = LO_GRID.
    ENDIF.

    CALL METHOD LO_GRID->CHECK_CHANGED_DATA.

    LOOP AT LO_REPORT->IT_FINAL  INTO DATA(WA_FINAL) WHERE CHECK = 'X'.
      IF WA_FINAL-CREATED_BY IS  INITIAL .
        WA_BAPIRET2-TYPE ='E'.
        WA_BAPIRET2-MESSAGE = |Please Maintain Created By For Material Doc { WA_FINAL-MBLNR }|.
        APPEND WA_BAPIRET2 TO IT_BAPIRET2 .
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF .
      IF WA_FINAL-VERIFIED_BY IS  INITIAL .
        WA_BAPIRET2-TYPE ='E'.
        WA_BAPIRET2-MESSAGE = |Please Maintain Verified By For Material Doc { WA_FINAL-MBLNR }|.
        APPEND WA_BAPIRET2 TO IT_BAPIRET2 .
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF .
    ENDLOOP.
    "perform something.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi @Archie_Saxena 

deleting or changing an In-House Repair especially regarding sold-to party is currently not possible. However, you can set the wrong in-house repair immediately to completed, and by default completed in-house repairs are not displayed in the result list.

Best Regards,
Udo
